modalWidth.title: Modal Width
modalWidth.helper: Constrain the maximum width of all modal dialogs. Affects settings, editors, and other popover panels.

drawer.title: Drawer
drawer.showTabLabelsHint: Display short names below each tab icon in the sidebar

chatHeads.title: Chat Heads
chatHeads.showHint: Display floating indicators for background generations
chatHeads.completionSoundHint: Play a ding when a background chat head finishes generating

completionSound.fileTooLarge: Audio file must be 2MB or smaller
completionSound.blocked: Browser blocked audio playback — interact with the page first
completionSound.uploadHint: Upload an MP3, WAV, OGG, AAC or M4A file (max 2MB) to replace the default ding.

landing.title: Pagination
landing.layoutHelper: Switch the landing page between the current card gallery and a denser adaptive list of recent chats.

notifications.unsupported: Push notifications are not supported in this browser.
notifications.generationFailedHint: When a generation encounters an error
notifications.toastEnabled: Push notifications enabled for this device
notifications.failDisabled: Push notifications are disabled in settings
notifications.failUserActive: Push notifications are suppressed while you are actively viewing Lumiverse

voice.enableTtsHint: Allow AI responses to be spoken aloud
voice.autoPlayHint: Automatically speak AI responses when generation completes
voice.ttsTestPhrase: Hello! This is a test of the text-to-speech system.
voice.separateNarratorHint: Speak narration/thoughts in a different voice from character speech. Applies anywhere narration appears (single-character cards too).
voice.separateNarratorSpeedHint: Speak narration at its own pace instead of inheriting the Speed setting above.
voice.sttContinuousHint: Keep listening after each silence-delimited result
voice.sttInterimHint: Display partial transcriptions as you speak
voice.sttAutoSubmitWebHint: Stop Web Speech recognition after a sustained pause
voice.sttAutoSubmitConnHint: Wait for a sustained pause before transcribing and queueing
voice.sttMicButton: Show mic button in input bar
voice.sttMicButtonHint: Display the speech-to-text shortcut beside the message input
voice.sttWebSpeechUnavailable: Web Speech API is not available in this browser. Try Chrome or Edge, or switch to an STT connection.
voice.sttConnectionFallback: Speech-to-text uses a dedicated STT connection from Connections settings.

mcp.name: Name
mcp.headersHint: Custom headers (API keys, auth tokens). Values are stored encrypted.
mcp.commandHint: The executable to run (e.g. node, python3, npx)
mcp.envHint: Env vars passed to the subprocess. Values are stored encrypted.
mcp.noServersHint: Add one to enable external tool access.
mcp.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This will disconnect and remove the server configuration.

tokenizers.configsDesc: Tokenizer engines used for counting tokens. Paste a HuggingFace model URL to add one automatically. Built-in configs cannot be deleted.
tokenizers.patternsDesc: Regex patterns that map model IDs to tokenizers. Higher priority matched first.
tokenizers.testTokenizerDesc: Count tokens for sample text using a specific tokenizer.
tokenizers.testPatternDesc: See which tokenizer would be used for a given model ID.
tokenizers.verified: Verified — tokenizer loads and counts tokens
tokenizers.autoMatchToggle: Automatically use this tokenizer for matching model IDs
tokenizers.advanced: Advanced — add manually (OpenAI / custom tiktoken / approximate)
tokenizers.advancedDesc: Enter a config JSON directly. Use this for non-HuggingFace tokenizers, e.g. {"encoding":"cl100k_base"} or {"charsPerToken":4}.
tokenizers.hfDesc: Store a HuggingFace token to read gated or private model repos. The token is encrypted at rest and only ever sent to HuggingFace.
tokenizers.hfConfigured: Token saved — gated & private repos enabled.
tokenizers.hfPlaceholderSet: •••••••• (saved — enter a new token to replace)

chat.title: Chat
chat.bubbleAvatarBg: Show character art in bubble backgrounds
chat.bubbleAvatarBgHint: Uses the message avatar as a subtle dissolving background in Bubble mode
chat.widthHelper: Constrain the chat message area width. Useful for ultrawide monitors where full-width messages stretch too far.
chat.messagesPerPageHelper: How many messages to load at a time. Lower values improve loading speed on slow connections.
chat.saveDraftHint: Automatically saves your unsent message so it persists across page refreshes and chat switches
chat.regenHelper: When enabled, a feedback prompt appears before each regeneration or swipe, letting you guide the next response.
chat.regenHintUser: Feedback is appended to the last user message as [OOC: ...]
chat.regenHintSystem: Feedback is appended to the end of the system prompt as [OOC: ...]
chat.showTokenCount: Show token count in message pill
chat.showTokenCountHint: Displays the token count for assistant messages in the timestamp badge below each message
chat.contextMenu: Enable right-click / long-press menu on messages
chat.contextMenuHint: When off, the in-app context menu won't appear — useful on mobile if you'd rather use the OS's native long-press to select and copy text without competing with the in-app menu.
chat.swipeHelper: Navigate message swipes using touch gestures (mobile) or arrow keys (desktop). Hold Shift and hover to target a specific message.

extensions.title: Extension Settings
extensions.placeholder: Installed extensions can expose configuration controls here.
extensions.noFrontend: No frontend-capable extensions are currently installed.

guided.title: Guided Generations
guided.helper: Attach reusable prompts as system content or user prefixes/suffixes. Drag the handle to reorder.

quickReplies.title: Quick Replies
quickReplies.helper: Build your own quick-reply sets for the input bar popover.
quickReplies.empty: No quick reply sets configured yet.
quickReplies.label: Label

extensionPools.title: Extension Ephemeral Pools
extensionPools.errGlobalMax: Global max bytes must be a positive number
extensionPools.errExtDefault: Per-extension default max bytes must be a positive number
extensionPools.errTtl: Reservation TTL must be a positive number
extensionPools.errPassword: Owner password is required to save pool changes
extensionPools.fitsExtensions: Fits roughly {{count}} extension(s) at the default cap.
extensionPools.warnExceedsGlobal: Warning: per-extension default exceeds global cap.
extensionPools.overrides: PER-EXTENSION OVERRIDES (identifier=value per line, optional suffix b/mb/gb)

webSearch.title: Web Search
webSearch.helper: Configure a SearXNG instance for the host-backed council web-search tool. The tool will appear in Council once search is enabled and an API URL is configured.
webSearch.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste a new key to replace
webSearch.enginesHint: Optional comma-separated engine allowlist passed through to SearXNG.
webSearch.testQueryPlaceholder: Enter a query to validate this setup
webSearch.saveFailed: Failed to save web search settings
webSearch.testSuccess: Web search test passed. Retrieved {{results}} results and {{pages}} extracted pages.
webSearch.testFormHint: Testing uses the current form values, including any unsaved API key override, so you can validate the instance before saving.

lumihub.title: LumiHub
lumihub.helper: Link this Lumiverse instance to LumiHub to install characters directly from the web.
lumihub.manifestText: A basic manifest of your installed characters (names and creators only) is synced to your LumiHub account to enable remote card updates. No chat data, messages, or personal content is ever shared. Lumiverse and LumiHub developers cannot access your data. Third-party LumiHub instances may have different privacy practices — exercise caution.
lumihub.instanceHint: A label to identify this instance on LumiHub (e.g. "Home PC", "Laptop")

dataPortability.exportDesc: Bundle everything you own — characters, chats, world books, personas, presets, memory cortex, databanks, themes, settings, and extension preferences — into a single portable archive (.lvbak). API keys and other secrets are never included.
dataPortability.includeVectors: Include vector embeddings (faster import on a matching embedding provider; larger file)
dataPortability.includeSecrets: Include API keys & secrets (downloads a separate decryption ticket)
dataPortability.secretsWarning: Two files will download: the archive (.lvbak) and a small ticket (.ticket.json). Keep the ticket separate from the archive — a password manager is ideal. Without the ticket, the keys cannot be restored. With both files together, anyone who obtains them can read your API keys.
dataPortability.exportSecretsWarn: {{count}} secret could not be decrypted on this server and was excluded from the archive. This usually means the row was written by an older identity key or was inserted manually. Affected key: {{keys}}
dataPortability.exportSecretsWarn_other: {{count}} secrets could not be decrypted on this server and were excluded from the archive. This usually means the row was written by an older identity key or was inserted manually. Affected keys: {{keys}}
dataPortability.importDesc: Restore a .lvbak archive into this account. Imports merge by ID — your existing data is preserved. You'll need to re-enter API keys after the import, since secrets are never carried in archives.
dataPortability.importWarn: The archive is processed in the background after upload. Don't close this tab until you see the completion summary — but it's safe to keep using Lumiverse while it runs.
dataPortability.ticketPrompt: This archive carries {{count}} encrypted secret. Upload your ticket file to restore them.
dataPortability.ticketPrompt_other: This archive carries {{count}} encrypted secrets. Upload your ticket file to restore them.
dataPortability.ticketReuse: Heads up: this ticket has been used {{uses}} time. Proceeding will overwrite any matching API keys on this account.
dataPortability.ticketReuse_other: Heads up: this ticket has been used {{uses}} times{{lastUsed}}. Proceeding will overwrite any matching API keys on this account.
dataPortability.ticketSkipped: Skipping API keys — applying rest of archive…
dataPortability.ticketInvalidJson: Ticket file is not valid JSON

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.loadFailed: Failed to load chat memory settings
advanced.warmup: Warm Long-Term Chat Memory when opening a chat
advanced.warmupHint: Opt-in automatic warmup. Manual rebuilds from the chat input bar still work even when this is off.
advanced.memoryModeHint: Quick presets auto-configure chunking & exclusion. "Manual" unlocks all fields below.
advanced.imgOptHelper: Control the resolution of generated thumbnail tiers. Smaller values reduce bandwidth; larger values improve visual quality.
advanced.smallTierHint: Cards, message avatars, and small UI elements. Default: 300px
advanced.largeTierHint: Portrait panel, character editor, and profile views. Default: 700px
advanced.rebuildCacheHint: Regenerate all thumbnails at the current tier sizes.
advanced.similarityHint: 0 = no filtering. Cosine distance can exceed 1, so useful cutoffs are not limited to 0–1.
advanced.formattingHelper: Templates control how retrieved memories appear in the prompt. Available placeholders: {{memories}}, {{content}}, {{score}}, {{startIndex}}, {{endIndex}}.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.reattributeDesc: Match user messages to persona avatars by name. Useful for chats imported from SillyTavern.
diagnostics.reattributeToastNone: All user messages are already attributed to personas
diagnostics.pushDisabled: Push notifications are disabled in settings.
diagnostics.pushEventDisabled: Generation completed notifications are disabled in settings.
diagnostics.pushUserActive: Push notifications are suppressed while you are actively viewing Lumiverse.
diagnostics.pushNoSubscriptions: No push subscriptions found. Subscribe in Notifications settings first.

migration.browseSubtitle: Choose how to connect to your SillyTavern installation, then navigate to the root directory containing the data/ folder.
migration.looksLikeFolder: Looks like a SillyTavern folder — click Validate to confirm
migration.stUserSubtitle: Your SillyTavern installation has multiple user profiles. Select which one to migrate.
migration.autoImportWarning: Characters will be auto-imported because chat history depends on them.
migration.tagLibraryHint: Upload a SillyTavern TagLibrary JSON backup to add tags after migration. Existing character tags are preserved.
migration.targetSubtitle: Select which Lumiverse account should receive the imported data.
migration.confirmSubtitle: Previously imported characters will be skipped automatically. This operation may take a while for large datasets.

connectionPicker.shareHint: The name of the shared folder (e.g. \\host\share)
connectionPicker.gdriveSetupHint: Connect to Google Drive using OAuth. Create a credential in the Google Cloud Console (APIs & Services > Credentials) with the Drive API enabled, then enter your Client ID below. A Client Secret is required for Web application credentials.
connectionPicker.clientSecret: Client Secret (required for Web app credentials)
connectionPicker.gdriveAuthorizeHint: Credentials saved. Click below to authorize Lumiverse to read your Google Drive files.
connectionPicker.dbxSetupHint: Connect to Dropbox. Create an app at dropbox.com/developers and enter your App Key below. No App Secret is needed.
connectionPicker.dbxAuthorizeHint1: Click below to open Dropbox authorization. You'll receive a code to paste back here.
connectionPicker.dbxAuthorizeHint2: A new tab has opened. Sign in to Dropbox, authorize the app, then paste the code below.
connectionPicker.authCodePlaceholder: Paste the code from Dropbox here

directoryBrowser.pathPlaceholderRemote: Enter remote path and press Enter

users.title: User Management
users.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete {{name}}? This cannot be undone.
users.deleteWiping: Wiping data for {{name}}. This can take a while if they have a lot of chats, vectors, or files.

memoryCortex.title: Memory Cortex
memoryCortex.enableHint: Adds entity tracking, importance scoring, and emotional recall on top of existing long-term memory. Your existing memory system continues working independently.
memoryCortex.warmup: Warm Memory Cortex when opening a chat
memoryCortex.warmupHint: Opt-in automatic warmup. Manual rebuilds from the chat input bar still work even when this is off.
memoryCortex.disabledNotice1: Memory Cortex is off. Your existing long-term chat memory (vector search) is still active.
memoryCortex.disabledNotice2: Enable the cortex to add entity tracking, narrative importance scoring, and emotionally-aware recall.
memoryCortex.presetSimpleDesc: One toggle. Smart defaults. Entity tracking and importance scoring on, everything else automatic.
memoryCortex.presetStandardDesc: Entities, relationships, emotional recall, and story arc summaries. Great for multi-session campaigns.
memoryCortex.presetAdvancedDesc: Full control over every parameter. Retrieval weights, decay curves, token budgets, and more.
memoryCortex.formattingSection: How memories appear in the story
memoryCortex.useLtmFormattingHint: Preserves Settings > Advanced > Long-Term Chat Memory > Formatting templates for the injected Long-Term Memory block. Turn off to use Cortex formatter modes below.
memoryCortex.loadFailed: Failed to load Memory Cortex settings
memoryCortex.openChatFirst: Open a chat first to rebuild its memory graph
memoryCortex.invalidTag: Tag names must be lowercase letters, digits, or underscores only.
memoryCortex.formatterShadowDesc: Prose-style context the AI weaves in naturally
memoryCortex.formatterClinicalDesc: Database-style facts and bullet points (for lore tracking)
memoryCortex.formatterMinimalDesc: Just memory chunks, no entity data
memoryCortex.thoughtMarkersHint: Memory Cortex uses these markers to classify colored text as thoughts instead of narration. Quoted dialogue is still detected automatically, and *asterisk-wrapped thoughts* remain supported.
memoryCortex.sidecarHint: Use a secondary LLM for deeper entity extraction, importance scoring, and memory consolidation. Without a connection, these features use fast heuristics (free, no API calls).
memoryCortex.sidecarReasoningBound: This sidecar connection uses bound reasoning settings: {{summary}}.
memoryCortex.sidecarReasoningGlobal: This sidecar connection uses the global Reasoning settings unless the connection profile has bound reasoning settings. Bind reasoning on that connection to force thinking on or off for Cortex extraction and summaries.
memoryCortex.modelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use connection default
memoryCortex.noModels: No models returned for this connection. Enter one manually.
memoryCortex.modelBrowseHint: Click into the field to browse models for the selected sidecar connection, or leave it blank to use the connection default.
memoryCortex.aiSummariesHint: Consolidation summaries written by AI instead of extractive selection
memoryCortex.fallbackSkip: AI Only — skip chunk, retry on warmup
memoryCortex.arbitratesHeuristicsHint: When the sidecar succeeds, it sees the heuristic candidates for this chunk and can drop bad ones or rename them to a canonical form before merging.
memoryCortex.pruneGraph: Sidecar can prune existing graph entities
memoryCortex.pruneGraphHint: The sidecar may flag entities already in the graph as invalid (e.g. a verb captured by an earlier heuristic pass). Flagged entities are deleted; user-edited entities are always preserved.
memoryCortex.sidecarLongHint: Max output tokens: ceiling on what the sidecar can emit per call. Each chunk's result JSON is ~400-600 tokens with arbiter on, so a chunks-per-request value of N needs roughly N × 600 tokens of headroom. If responses get truncated mid-JSON the whole batch falls back to per-chunk extraction — much slower. Raise this if you increase chunks per request. Chunks per request: how many memory chunks to analyze in a single LLM call. Higher = fewer API calls but larger prompts. Parallel requests: how many LLM calls to run simultaneously during rebuild. RPM limit: cap Cortex sidecar requests per minute for this provider. 0 disables throttling. Sidecar timeout: max wait per sidecar call. Increase for thinking/reasoning models that need more processing time. 0 = no limit. Retrieval timeout: max wait for cortex retrieval during generation. If exceeded, falls back to plain vector search. 0 = no limit. On sidecar failure: "AI Only" mode skips writing anything for this chunk (heuristic noise stays out of the graph) — the next cortex warmup will retry it. Retry attempts/delay: extra sidecar tries with exponential backoff before falling back. 0 = no retry (legacy behavior).
memoryCortex.whitelistHint: Words the entity extractor should never filter out. Use this for fantasy proper nouns that look like common words (e.g., "The Pale", "Binding", "Ash").
memoryCortex.whitelistPlaceholder: Type a term and press Enter...
memoryCortex.scaffoldHint: Tag names (XML/HTML-style, lowercase) whose inner content is structured scaffolding — HUD panels, status lines, dice rolls, embeds — that should be removed wholesale before entity extraction. Common ones (status, embed, hud, dice, tracker, ooc, tool_call, etc.) are stripped by default; add anything custom your cards use here. Example: "rpgstats", "encounter", "questlog".
memoryCortex.emotionalResonanceHint: Boost memories matching the current scene's emotional tone
memoryCortex.diversitySelectionHint: Prevent multiple retrieved memories from the same time period
memoryCortex.entitySnapshotsHint: Include character/location summaries in the prompt
memoryCortex.relationshipEdgesHint: Include character relationships in the prompt
memoryCortex.coreMemoryHint: Memories scoring above the threshold are "core memories" — they resist decay and never fully fade.
memoryCortex.enableConsolidationHint: Compress older memories into summaries. Reduces token usage for long campaigns.
memoryCortex.entityFiltersHint: Protected terms seed a specific memory type from matching lines. Rejected terms block that type from matching lines. Cleanup regexes run in order and remove matched text from protected lines before the entity is saved. Use JavaScript-style regex strings like /^.*📍\s*/.
memoryCortex.scaffoldAlready: "{{tag}}" is already in the scaffold list

operator.vectorResetSuccess: Vector store reset. It will reinitialize on next use.
operator.busyTogglingRemote: Toggling remote mode... Server will restart.
operator.confirmRestartMessage: Restart the Lumiverse server? Your browser will temporarily disconnect and reconnect automatically.
operator.confirmRebuildMessage: This will delete the frontend build, rebuild it from source, and restart the server. Your browser will temporarily disconnect.
operator.dbMaintenanceSuccess: Database tuning refreshed and optimize completed.
operator.sharpResetSuccess: Sharp runtime settings reset to defaults.
operator.remoteAcceptAny: Connections accepted from any origin. Disable when not needed.
operator.vacuumEligible: Eligible to run on the next scheduler pass.
operator.ipcNotStarted: Runner IPC not available. This server was started without the runner. Start Lumiverse with ./start.sh in an interactive terminal, or use bun run runner.
operator.ipcNoProcessSend: Runner IPC was requested, but Bun did not expose process.send in this server process. This usually means the server was relaunched outside the runner IPC channel.
operator.confirmUpdateMessage: This will pull the latest changes ({{count}} commits), reinstall dependencies, rebuild the frontend, and restart the server. Your browser will temporarily disconnect.
operator.confirmSwitchBranchMessage: Switch to the "{{branch}}" branch? This will checkout, pull, reinstall, rebuild, and restart the server.
operator.confirmShutdownP1: This will completely stop the Lumiverse server and runner. You will need to restart manually from the terminal.
operator.confirmShutdownP2: This action cannot be undone from the web interface.
operator.confirmRemoteP1: Remote mode allows connections from any origin. This is intended for accessing Lumiverse from other devices on your network or remotely.
operator.confirmRemoteP2: Enabling remote mode exposes your Lumiverse instance to any device that can reach this server. Only enable this if you understand the security implications and trust your network.
operator.confirmRemoteP3: The server will restart to apply this change.
operator.remoteLocalOnly: Only local and LAN connections are accepted.
operator.reconnectingHint: The server is restarting. This page will refresh automatically once it's back.
operator.shutdownHint: Lumiverse has been stopped. Restart it from the terminal to continue.
operator.confirmVectorResetMessage: This will delete the entire LanceDB directory, clear all cached embeddings, and reset vectorization flags. The vector store will reinitialize automatically on next use.
operator.logsEmpty: No log entries yet. Server output will appear here.
operator.hostsHint: Hostnames accepted in the HTTP Host header. Add the DNS or Tailscale name you use to reach this device. Wildcards are not allowed — this guards against DNS-rebinding attacks.
operator.hostsRefreshFailed: Trusted hostname refresh failed: {{error}}. Showing the last successful snapshot.
operator.hostsDetectFailed: Trusted hostname detection is still unavailable: {{error}}
operator.hostsNotLoaded: Trusted hostname data has not loaded yet.
operator.hostsPortHint: Port defaults to {{port}} when omitted. Full https://*.ts.net origins are preserved for Tailscale Serve.
operator.hostsLoadSnapshotHint: Load the current snapshot before editing so existing trusted hosts are preserved.
operator.dbDiskNotEnough: not enough free space for a safe vacuum
operator.dbVacuumUnsafe: SQLite VACUUM is currently unsafe: estimated rewrite headroom is {{needed}}, but only {{free}} appears free on this volume.
operator.dbCachePercentHint: Blank uses the automatic cache target. Current recommendation: {{size}}.
operator.dbMmapHint: Linux/macOS only. Blank uses the automatic mmap target. Set 0 to disable mmap explicitly.
operator.dbIdleSignalsValue: visible sessions {{visible}}/{{total}} · hidden for {{hidden}} min · last write {{lastWrite}} · active generations {{generations}}
operator.dbEnableAutoVacuumHint: Runs only when the interval, idle, and reclaim thresholds are all satisfied.
operator.confirmVacuumP1: This will checkpoint the WAL, rewrite the SQLite database, reclaim free pages, run ANALYZE, and finish with PRAGMA optimize.
operator.confirmVacuumScratch: Estimated scratch space needed: {{needed}} · currently free: {{free}}
operator.confirmVacuumReclaim: Reclaimable space right now: {{reclaimable}} · active generations: {{generations}}
operator.confirmVacuumP2: This can block writes while the file is rebuilt. Run it when the server is otherwise quiet.
operator.sharpConcurrencyHint: Blank uses the backend default. New image work picks up changes immediately.
operator.vectorNotInit: No vector store initialized. It will be created automatically when embeddings are first used.

embeddings.title: Embeddings
embeddings.helper: Configure vector embeddings for long-term memory retrieval. Vectorizes world books, chat messages, and documents for vector search during generation.
embeddings.inheritedNotice: These embedding settings are managed by the server owner. Your account inherits the shared configuration and uses the owner's API key — you cannot change the provider, model, or key here. Test verifies the inherited connection works for your account.
embeddings.testSuccess: Embedding test passed. Dimensions set to {{dims}}.
embeddings.setupReady: Vector search is ready for world books.
embeddings.checkEnabledDesc: Turns on vector features across Lumiverse.
embeddings.checkApiKeyDesc: Lets the app reach your embedding provider.
embeddings.checkDimensionsDesc: Run a test once so Lumiverse knows the vector size.
embeddings.checkWorldBookDesc: Allows lorebook entries to be indexed and searched with vectors.
embeddings.noModels: No models returned for this provider. Enter one manually.
embeddings.browseHint: Click into the field to browse embedding-capable models for this provider, or type one manually.
embeddings.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste a new key to replace
embeddings.dimensionsHint: Run Test to auto-detect from your model.
embeddings.worldBookHelper: Controls how lorebook entries are chunked and retrieved. Saves automatically.
embeddings.presetLeanDesc: Smaller chunks and lighter storage for compact lorebooks.
embeddings.presetBalancedDesc: Recommended. Better coverage without blowing up index size.
embeddings.presetDeepDesc: More chunks and broader recall for dense reference books.
embeddings.presetCustomDesc: Tune chunking, recall, and storage manually.
embeddings.testInheritedHint: Testing verifies the inherited embedding connection without changing shared provider settings. World-book chunking and retrieval tuning still save automatically.
embeddings.testHint: Testing auto-detects native model dimensions and applies them to this configuration. World-book chunking and retrieval tuning save automatically.
embeddings.sendDimensionsHint: When enabled, the dimensions value above is included in the embedding API request. Some providers set this automatically from the model and may reject an explicit value.
embeddings.apiUrlPathHint: Auto-appends /v1/embeddings to base domains and /embeddings to partial paths. Full paths ending in /embeddings are used as-is.
embeddings.bananabreadHint: BananaBread defaults to `http://localhost:8008/v1/embeddings` and uses its loaded model list from `/v1/models`.
embeddings.worldBooksCardMeta: Chunking and storage save automatically for your account.
embeddings.presetEditSwitch: Editing any value below switches to Custom.
embeddings.rerankCutoffHint: 0 disables post-rerank filtering after boosts and penalties are applied.
embeddings.batchSizeHint: Entries or chunks embedded per request during indexing.
